$comment: Single source of truth for ALL branding. Edit this, run `pnpm brand:apply`, and every consumer (Capacitor configs, electron-builder, Info.plist, entitlements, web manifest, Swift constants, root package.json, README badges) is regenerated. Adding a new consumer? Add a function to scripts/native/apply-brand.mjs.
name: heron
description: Heron is a thinking partner for career transitions. Local-first job-search platform: pipeline tracking, A–F role evaluation, ATS-optimized CVs, 11-portal scanning, recruiter email triage, interview prep, and opt-in autonomous apply. Runs on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, iPadOS, Android, and Apple Watch.

identifiers.$comment: capacitorPluginName: bridge contract between TS (registerPlugin) and Swift (jsName). Must match exactly on both sides; apply-brand generates BRAND.capacitorPluginName + Brand.capacitorPluginName so a rebrand retargets both consumers in one step.

extensions.$comment: Xcode extension targets — generic structural identifiers. Folder paths and target names are brand-agnostic (Extensions/AppWidget, Extensions/AppLiveActivity, Extensions/AppShareExtension), so a rebrand never touches Xcode targets. Bundle IDs flow from identifiers.bundleId + bundleSuffix (e.g. com.heron.app.widget). The deploymentMin floor is the platform minimum, not a brand decision.

colors.$comment: 9 base hex values + their human-readable names. The full 22-token CSS system in ui/src/app.css is generated from these by apply-brand (status colors + text scale are static across brands; everything else derives).

tokens.$comment: 22-token CSS system, derived from the 9 bases. apply-brand writes these into the AUTO-GENERATED block in ui/src/app.css. The shadcn-shaped token graph (--background, --foreground, --primary, --accent, --sidebar-*, status.*) maps onto the brand palette here. light = warm-paper register, dark = slate-tinted black register.

fonts.$comment: Typography system. apply-brand generates the @font-face declarations and the cascade defaults (body=sans, h1-h4=display, code/.font-mono=mono) into ui/src/app.css AUTO-GENERATED block. woff2 files live at ui/static/fonts/ — self-hosted, no CDN.

display.fallback: 'Iowan Old Style', 'Apple Garamond', Baskerville, 'Times New Roman', Times, Georgia, serif

body.fallback: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif

mono.fallback: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace

fonts.japaneseFallback: 'Hiragino Sans', 'Hiragino Kaku Gothic Pro', 'Yu Gothic', 'Yu Gothic UI', 'Meiryo', 'Noto Sans JP', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif

subsetUnicodeRanges.$comment: Unicode ranges for the @font-face declarations. Pulled from Google Fonts CSS output. Keeping them in brand.json means swapping in a new font (or a new subset) doesn't require an apply-brand code change.

voice.$comment: Brand voice as data — consumed by AUTO-GENERATED:<section> markers inside the .md docs. Narrative .md files (BRAND, VOICE, COLORS, TYPOGRAPHY, MASCOT, SOCIAL-CARD, PRESS, REBRAND-PROCESS) carry markers that apply-brand fills from this block + the rest of brand.json.
voice.subline: A thinking partner for career transitions. Patient, precise, local-first.
voice.origin: The heron stands motionless in shallow water. It waits. It watches. It evaluates every passing form. Then, when the moment is exactly right, it strikes — once, precisely, and the work is done.
voice.mission: Help one person make one excellent career move, instead of fifty mediocre ones. The wrong era for spray-and-pray is the era we're in.
voice.philosophy: Recruiters' attention is finite. So is yours. Quality over quantity, by design — the system actively discourages applications below a 4/5 score.

principles.name: Specific over abstract
principles.description: Concrete numbers, named tools, real workflows. Not 'AI-powered' or 'next-generation'.

principles.name: Quiet over loud
principles.description: No exclamation marks. No urgency-by-typography. Calm carries weight.

principles.name: Earned over claimed
principles.description: Don't say 'easy' — show the four-line quickstart. Don't say 'powerful' — show the feature.

principles.name: Patient over urgent
principles.description: Heron is for considered decisions. Manufactured urgency contradicts the brand at every layer.

principles.name: Local-first over cloud-default
principles.description: Data on disk. AI bring-your-own-key. No cloud aggregator.

principles.name: Filter over cannon
principles.description: The autonomous-apply gate is below 4/5 by default. The brand promise made operational.

antiPatterns.name: Hustle-bro tone
antiPatterns.example: "Crush your job search! 🚀" → no. "A thinking partner for career transitions." → yes.

antiPatterns.name: Empty futurism
antiPatterns.example: "Welcome to the future of work!" → no. (just describe what the thing does)

antiPatterns.name: Manufactured urgency
antiPatterns.example: "Get started in seconds!" / "Limited time!" → no. Time is the user's.

antiPatterns.name: Wellness coddling
antiPatterns.example: "You've got this! 💪" → no. The user is an adult.

antiPatterns.name: Easy claims
antiPatterns.example: "It's easy!" → never. Show the four lines, let them judge.

antiPatterns.name: Welcoming language
antiPatterns.example: "Welcome to Heron!" / "Let's get started!" — no. Open with what the page is for.

antiPatterns.name: Just-doing
antiPatterns.example: "Just paste the URL" — no. "Paste the URL." The word 'just' minimizes.

antiPatterns.name: We-language
antiPatterns.example: "We think you'll love it!" → no. The brand is the brand, not the maintainer.

boilerplate.short: Heron — a thinking partner for career transitions. Patient. Precise. Local-first. Open source.
boilerplate.medium: Heron is an open-source job-search assistant for the wrong era of spray-and-pray. It tracks your pipeline, scores every role A-F, generates ATS-optimized CVs, scans 11 ATSes, and triages recruiter email — all locally. Your data never leaves your machine.
boilerplate.long: The heron stands motionless in shallow water. It waits. It watches. It evaluates every passing form. Then, when the moment is exactly right, it strikes — once, precisely, and the work is done. Heron is a thinking partner for people in career transition who'd rather make one excellent move than fifty mediocre ones. It runs entirely on your machine: pipeline tracking, A-F role evaluation, ATS-optimized CVs, 11-portal scanning, recruiter email triage, interview prep, and opt-in autonomous apply. Open source. AI-agnostic. Your data stays yours.

quotes: Recruiters' attention is finite. So is yours. The wrong era for spray-and-pray is the era we're in. Heron is the alternative.
Local-first isn't a feature, it's a posture. Your career data is the most concentrated personal data you'll ever generate. Treating it like analytics fodder for a SaaS isn't a tradeoff we made.
We score every role before applying. Below four out of five, the system actively discourages you. The recruiter's time is worth as much as yours.
The heron stands still. Then it strikes. That's the whole product, in two sentences.

mascot.$comment: Mascot identity — fed into the MASCOT.md template + apply-brand placeholder SVG generator. The SVG art itself lives in branding/assets/ (mark-placeholder.svg today, mark-{slate,light,dawn}.svg when the illustrator output lands).
mascot.subject: Great Blue Heron
mascot.pose: side profile, standing in shallow water, neck S-curve, head turned 25-30° right

mark.use: ≤64px contexts — favicon, app icon, inline UI

splash.$comment: Heron darkBg — must match capacitor.config.ts → plugins.SplashScreen.backgroundColor so the launch-screen storyboard and the @capacitor/splash-screen overlay look identical.

originalAuthor.name: santifer
originalAuthor.comment: Original creator of the upstream pre-fork project — Heron builds on their work. Credited in the README Acknowledgements section and via the upstream repository link from every issue page. Do not remove.

permissions.localNetworkUsage: Heron uses local network discovery to find your desktop app on the same Wi-Fi network so your phone, watch, and laptop stay in sync without a cloud account.
permissions.faceIdUsage: Heron uses Face ID to unlock sensitive screens — interview notes, negotiation playbooks, and offer comparisons — so a glance over your shoulder never leaks personal data.
permissions.cameraUsage: Heron uses the camera so you can scan business cards from interviews directly into your contacts file.
permissions.microphoneUsage: Heron uses the microphone for the mock-interview rehearsal mode — audio is processed on-device and never uploaded.
permissions.photoLibraryUsage: Heron needs photo-library access to attach screenshots of job postings or reference letters to a job entry.
permissions.calendarsUsage: Heron reads your calendar to surface upcoming interviews and to schedule follow-up reminders.
permissions.remindersUsage: Heron creates reminders for follow-ups, application deadlines, and interview prep blocks.
permissions.contactsUsage: Heron reads contacts so you can send outreach to recruiters and hiring managers you already know.
permissions.userTrackingUsage: Heron never tracks you. This prompt only appears because Apple requires it for any app that can render web content — we always respond with denied.

copyright: © 2026 Heron contributors (original work © 2026 santifer). Licensed under the MIT License.

appStore.subtitle: A thinking partner for careers
appStore.reviewerNotes: Heron is a personal job-search productivity tool. The app reads job postings the user pastes, drafts tailored CVs/cover letters locally, and tracks application status in a local SQLite database. No personal data leaves the device unless the user explicitly signs in with their own LLM API key (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google). Demo account: not applicable — the app is single-user / self-hosted by design.
appStore.promotionalText: Stand still. Strike well. A patient, local-first thinking partner for career transitions — your data stays on your device.
appStore.marketingText: Tailored CVs, 11-portal scanning, recruiter triage, interview prep. One offline-first dashboard for the moves that matter.

playStore.shortDescription: A thinking partner for career transitions. Local-first, AI-agnostic, private.
playStore.fullDescription: Heron is a patient, local-first thinking partner for career transitions. Tailor CVs and cover letters per role with on-device LLMs, scan 11+ job portals (LinkedIn, Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, Workday, …), track every application in a local SQLite database, and prep interviews with mock-question + STAR-story drills. Data stays on your device unless you sign in with your own LLM API key. Open-source, MIT-licensed.
